label: Basaltwind Saltway
description: Basaltwind Saltway serves as the primary interior passage linking the vessel’s sealed compartments, cut low and narrow within the dark, salt-scoured hull. The bulkheads are clad in corrosion-tolerant plating scored by mineral abrasion, while recessed service conduits run in straight, lidded channels to preserve watertight integrity. Ceiling lamps sit behind thickened glass to withstand constant grit, casting a hard, pale light that emphasizes the ship’s austere fittings. Flush deck plates and minimal handholds reflect The Basaltwind’s deliberate preference for sealed hardware over ornament in Brineshade’s saline basins.
